trivia_bullets.text: Memorial to Genius is the blue member of the five-card Memorial cycle from Dominaria (April 27, 2018). Thematically, a 'Memorial to Genius' on Dominaria reads as an elegy for Urza-era intellectual ambition and its catastrophic costs — the Tolarian Academy was destroyed by Urza's time-travel experiments and rebuilt as Tolaria West in the post-Mending era.
sources: WebSearch: Dominaria lore article via coolstuffinc.com

trivia_bullets.text: The {4}{U} sacrifice cost to draw two cards is the most mana-intensive activation in the Memorial cycle, limiting play. EDHREC rank 9095, appearing in approximately 20,052 Commander decks — second-lowest rank among the BBL-held Memorials.
Illustrated by James Paick, who also painted Memorial to Glory (no. 244) — the only artist to illustrate two Memorials in the cycle.

Community sentiment on MTGSalvation described Memorial to Genius as excellent for control decks 'trying to edge out close late games' as a colored-mana-producing draw sink, but the EDHREC Dominaria set review cautioned players are 'probably better off with a similar land that taps for colorless but doesn't enter the battlefield tapped.'
sources: Community sentiment — EDHREC Dominaria set review article

related_cards.name: Memorial to Glory
related_cards.note: white cycle member; same artist (James Paick); BBL holds

related_cards.name: Memorial to Unity
related_cards.note: green cycle member; not in BBL corpus

related_cards.name: Tolaria West
related_cards.note: named for the rebuilt Tolarian Academy; thematic anchor for Dominaria genius-memorial lore
